name: Louis C.K.
bio: Louis C.K. is an American stand-up comedian, writer, and filmmaker known for self-deprecating, observational material on parenting, mortality, and everyday frustration. He wrote for David Letterman, Conan OBrien, and Chris Rock before breaking out with specials such as Chewed Up, Hilarious, and Live at the Beacon Theater, and created the FX series Louie. In 2017 he admitted to multiple incidents of sexual misconduct after reporting by The New York Times; he later returned to touring and recording.

timeline.ev: Releases breakthrough special Shameless on HBO
Self-distributes Live at the Beacon Theater online
Admits to sexual misconduct following New York Times reporting

accolades: Grammy Award for Best Comedy Album, Sincerely Louis C.K. (2022)

bits.t: Dancing Is The Worst Possible Career Choice
